---
pagetitle: "jstats:How-Tos"
title-prefix: ""
---

## How-Tos

These how-tos assume you've worked through [Get Started](start-here.qmd) — you'll be
comfortable loading jstats, using `juse()` and `jload()`, and running the basic
functions. Each one stands on its own, so read whichever you need; for now,
[Get Started](start-here.qmd) is the place to begin.

The articles below are on the way. They go beyond the Get Started on-ramp and point
toward the fuller treatment in the books.

## Working with data from commercial statistical software

*Coming soon* — importing and working with data from SPSS, Stata, and SAS, including
value and variable labels and the different missing-value conventions.

## Building and checking a scale

*Coming soon* — combining Likert items into a scale and checking its reliability with
Cronbach's alpha.

## Handling missing values across platforms

*Coming soon* — declaring, converting, and preserving user-defined missing values as
data move between SPSS, Stata, SAS, and R.
